title: senecio johnstonii subsp. elgonensis
intro: Senecio johnstonii subsp. elgonensis, also known as the Elgon Groundsel, is a subspecies of the Senecio johnstonii plant, which is a member of the daisy family and is native to Kenya. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a purgative and an emetic, helping to induce vomiting and relieve constipation. The health benefits of Senecio johnstonii subsp. elgonensis include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and help with urinary issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and sometimes used as a powder or tincture, although its use and preparation should be consulted with a medical professional due to its potential toxicity.
